new_tokens.name: --border-soft
new_tokens.usage_note: Soft inner border one step fainter than --border. Replaces scoped --prv2-border-soft/--af-border/--xv1-border-soft definitions (style.css 369, 1430, 3443, 3986, 4049) and direct uses (3288, 4104). CAUTION: style.css:514 (.prv2-context border-left) and saites.html.j2:805 already reference var(--border-soft) which was UNDEFINED (fell back to currentColor / nothing) — defining this token silently changes those two borders; verify visually in both themes.

new_tokens.name: --text-soft
new_tokens.usage_note: Serif body text tier between --text and --text-muted (prv2 summary/stance at 507/576; statistika-detail tooltip footerColor #c9cdd9 reads this via getComputedStyle — 1-unit dark drift from #c9cdd9 to #c8ccd8 is imperceptible). Light 10.6:1.

new_tokens.name: --text-dim
new_tokens.usage_note: Dimmest text tier (ranks, counts, disabled) — promotes scoped --xv1-text-dim (style.css 3449) to :root; also personas.html.j2 89/136. Light value is deliberately sub-AA (3.65:1) — de-emphasized non-essential text only, never body copy.

new_tokens.name: --gold-weekly
new_tokens.usage_note: Weekly-brief type label (style.css 1354). Light value matches the mustard --yellow family, 4.65:1 on surface.

new_tokens.name: --surface-deep
new_tokens.usage_note: Deep panel gradient terminus between --bg and --surface (hero-v2 2969, week-strip 5534). Light = between paper bg and surface2.

new_tokens.name: --surface-lift
new_tokens.usage_note: Faint lift on surfaces — white-alpha on dark, ink-alpha on paper (white-alpha is invisible on cream). style.css 515, 2977 (hero dot-grid); saites.html.j2 805 (JS-built konteksta piezīme bg).

new_tokens.name: --hover-glow
new_tokens.usage_note: vote-filter-btn hover glow. Ink-alpha in light so the glow survives on paper.

new_tokens.name: --overlay-hover
new_tokens.usage_note: Active/hover overlay on chrome rows (statistika-detail.html.j2:108 .stat-range-btn.active).

new_tokens.name: --media-backdrop
new_tokens.usage_note: analysis-feat-media letterbox (style.css 1448). Light value assumes featured images are cream engravings (palette audit's reading of docs/featured-images) — see open question 5 if any are dark-generated.

new_tokens.name: --shadow-overlay
new_tokens.usage_note: Dropdown/menu elevation (style.css 231, 5357) + saites D3 node drop-shadow (saites.html.j2:380 via cssVar). Dark value preserved byte-exact; light = warm umber per palette mandate (paper casts warm shadows). Audit marked these same_in_light=true but the palette explicitly overrides — see open question 8.

new_tokens.name: --shadow-overlay-strong
new_tokens.usage_note: nav-more-menu (5327) and sgv1 suggestions dropdown (5409) elevation.

new_tokens.name: --shadow-card-hover
new_tokens.usage_note: vote-card hover lift (1075).

new_tokens.name: --shadow-scroll-fade
new_tokens.usage_note: xv1-select-clear sticky scroll fade (3764).

new_tokens.name: --hover-brighten
new_tokens.usage_note: Hover filter for xv1 items (3847). Brighten-on-hover reads as wash-out on paper — light flips to darken. var() inside filter is valid: filter: var(--hover-brighten).

new_tokens.name: --hover-brighten-soft
new_tokens.usage_note: xv1-item-topic hover (3897) + hero-search-btn hover (5402; 1.12→1.1 dark shift is imperceptible).

new_tokens.name: --red-bright
new_tokens.usage_note: red-500 family, distinct from --red #e25b5b in dark (do NOT merge dark values). Covers style.css 1842/1854/2811, templates (about 283, finanses 94, partija 100, saites 32/293/388/787/870/932/963) and bmv1.js stat/segment classes. In LIGHT both --red and --red-bright resolve to #b42318 — intentional unification on paper (5.73:1). Saites disagree-edge rgba(239,68,68,0.3) derived in JS as cssVar('--red-bright')+'4D'.

new_tokens.name: --red-soft
new_tokens.usage_note: stat-change/stat-hero-change negative text (statistika.html.j2:69, statistika-detail.html.j2:39). #f87171 is 2.5:1 on paper — must darken.

new_tokens.name: --blue
new_tokens.usage_note: Legacy info-blue base hue: bill-pill text (style.css 5021), stat numbers (about 263), rail dots (personas 89), Chart.js bars/lines (index 474/498, analizes 247/264/283). MUST stay 6-digit hex — JS concatenates alpha suffixes (cssVar('--blue')+'1A' replaces rgba(59,130,246,0.1) chart fills). Light #1d4ed8 = 5.84:1. The rgba(59,130,246,*) tint family in style.css stays literal (same_in_light) — see open question 2.

new_tokens.name: --purple
new_tokens.usage_note: Violet for vote-alignment edges/cards (saites 44/73/869/932/934, about 278) AND the lēmumprojekts pill (style.css 5022 — folds #a855f7 into #8b5cf6, a small DARK-mode shift, see open question 3). 6-digit hex required: saites derives edge alphas as cssVar('--purple')+'80' (agree) / +'40' (neutral). Light 6.20:1.

new_tokens.name: --domain-economy
new_tokens.usage_note: Statistika domain accent. Decoupled from --accent so the economy hue stays steel while --accent goes navy in light. 6-digit hex (JS appends '33'/'2E' gradient alphas). Light 5.46:1 as chip text.

new_tokens.name: --domain-social
new_tokens.usage_note: Statistika social domain (statistika 52/153, statistika-detail 46/57/247). 6-digit hex for alpha concat. Light 5.43:1.

new_tokens.name: --domain-prices
new_tokens.usage_note: Alias — auto-themes with --orange (#f97316 → #b8400c). getComputedStyle resolves the alias to the concrete hex, so JS alpha concat still works.

new_tokens.name: --domain-state
new_tokens.usage_note: Alias — auto-themes with --green (#22c55e → #1a7a3a).

new_tokens.name: --chart-grid
new_tokens.usage_note: Chart.js gridline color (statistika-detail 338/343). White-alpha inverts badly on paper.

new_tokens.name: --chart-axis
new_tokens.usage_note: Chart.js axis border (statistika-detail 339).

new_tokens.name: --tooltip-bg
new_tokens.usage_note: Chart.js tooltip background (statistika 182, statistika-detail 316). Merges the surface-based and bg-based dark variants into the surface-based one — imperceptible dark shift on the detail page.

new_tokens.name: --tooltip-border
new_tokens.usage_note: Tooltip + saites legend overlay hairline (statistika 184, statistika-detail 319, saites 59).

new_tokens.name: --graph-legend-bg
new_tokens.usage_note: Saites D3 graph legend overlay scrim (saites.html.j2:59).

new_tokens.name: --graph-label
new_tokens.usage_note: D3 surname labels + legend text (saites 59, 411).

new_tokens.name: --graph-node-stroke
new_tokens.usage_note: D3 node circle stroke. Distinct from --hover-glow despite identical dark literal — different light semantics.

new_tokens.name: --graph-node-stroke-hover
new_tokens.usage_note: Ink in light, paper-white in dark.

new_tokens.name: --graph-link-default
new_tokens.usage_note: Default/unknown D3 edge hairline (saites:304).

new_tokens.name: --stat-hero-grad-start
new_tokens.usage_note: background-clip:text hero gradient — light flips to an ink gradient.

new_tokens.name: --stat-hero-grad-end
new_tokens.usage_note: Hero text gradient end stop.

new_tokens.name: --border-strong
new_tokens.usage_note: Palette-mandated NEW affordance token: interactive component outlines (inputs, focused chips, the theme switch) where WCAG 1.4.11 wants 3:1 (3.26:1 on paper). No existing literal replaced — adopt opportunistically.

new_tokens.name: --surface-hover
new_tokens.usage_note: Palette token: hover tint between surface and surface2. Pair with border darkening so the state change is never color-only. Available for future use; replaces nothing today.

new_tokens.name: --accent-brand-hover
new_tokens.usage_note: Brand navy hover step (lightens in dark, deepens in light). For the switch + brand-filled buttons.

new_tokens.name: --accent-highlight-hover
new_tokens.usage_note: Brand red hover step (8.13:1 in light).

new_tokens.name: --switch-plate
new_tokens.usage_note: Theme-switch wall plate.

new_tokens.name: --switch-outline
new_tokens.usage_note: 2px editorial ink stroke around plate/lever/screws — inverted engraving in dark.

new_tokens.name: --switch-lever
new_tokens.usage_note: Lever body — ink-black on cream plate in light, cream on dark plate in dark (~14:1 both).

new_tokens.name: --switch-lever-detail
new_tokens.usage_note: Lever slot/highlight line — the opposite ink (engraved bevel effect).

new_tokens.name: --switch-screw
new_tokens.usage_note: Screw-head fill; slot lines use --switch-outline.

template_groups.label: Group A — about.html.j2 (SVG pipeline diagram + stat cards)
template_groups.instructions: All edits in ~/atmina/templates/about.html.j2. (1) SVG diagram (L32-248): var() does NOT work inside SVG presentation attributes (fill="..."/stroke="...") — move each literal into the SVG's existing <style> block as a class rule, or convert the attribute to style="fill:var(--x)". For <stop> elements use style="stop-color:var(--surface)". Mappings: #161a22→var(--surface) (gradient stop L32; .claim-pill fill L52; .topic-bucket L53; panel rects L104/139/175); #0d1014→var(--bg) (gradient stop L33; .anim-pipeline-wrap background in the page <style> L379); #90A4AE→var(--accent) (arrowhead marker L37; .claim-pill stroke L52; doc-header/tspans/DB-bar fills L109/146/155/164/185/219/220/224); #8b8fa3→var(--text-muted) (.step-num/.step-sub/score/caption/header fills L44/49/181/188/195/233/241/248); #e2e4e9→var(--text) (.step-title L47 + pill texts L144/153/162/179/186/193/230); #2d3148→var(--border) (.doc-line L50; panel strokes L104/139/175); #B71C1C→var(--accent-highlight) (.doc-line-hl L51; marker rect L178; badge stroke L201); #37474F→var(--accent-brand) (connectors L92; topic-bucket stroke L215); #22c55e→var(--green) (Budžets marker L192). LEAVE AS LITERALS (theme-neutral, no token created): rgba(183,28,28,0.18) badge fill L200 and #C62828 badge text L202. (2) Stat-card inline styles: L263 #3b82f6→var(--blue); L268 #22c55e→var(--green); L278 #8b5cf6→var(--purple); L283 #ef4444→var(--red-bright). (3) Do NOT introduce new Jinja filters/globals (tests/test_topics.py + test_render_own_pubs.py build their own Jinja envs and would break). Verify the animated diagram visually in BOTH themes after the :root/light-block lands. Baseline regen (REGEN=1 pytest tests/test_render_chars.py) happens once centrally after all groups merge — do not regen inside this group.

template_groups.label: Group B — profile/list/finance pages (small inline fixes)
template_groups.instructions: personas.html.j2: L89 #3b82f6→var(--blue); L94 #8b8fa3→var(--text-muted); L99 #5e6478→var(--text-dim); L136 Jinja fallback --spine:{{ p.party_color or '#5e6478' }} → use 'var(--text-dim)' as the or-fallback (var() is valid inside an inline style custom-property). politician.html.j2: L599 + L604 mini saites-graph <circle stroke="#fff"> — remove the stroke attribute, add class="saites-node" to both circles, and add to the page's EXISTING inline <style> block: .mini-saites-graph .saites-node { stroke: var(--bg); } (the ring's job is separating the node from the page background — per JS audit). L591 Jinja party-color fallback '#8b8fa3' feeds an SVG fill presentation attribute — leave the literal (theme-neutral gray; var() would be invalid there). partija.html.j2 L100: #ef4444→var(--red-bright). finanses.html.j2 L94: #ef4444→var(--red-bright); NOTE the live finanses page is served from curated/atmina/finanses.html (frozen content) — edit the template anyway for source truth; the curated decision is tracked as an open question. balsojumi.html.j2 L444: 'var(--accent, #4a90d9)' → 'var(--accent)' (stale fallback sync, inside the inline <script>). 404.html.j2 was audited: its single #fff sits on an accent fill and is theme-neutral — no edit. Constraints: no new Jinja filters/globals (test_topics.py/test_render_own_pubs.py hand-built envs); baseline regen is centralized after merge.

js_plan.instructions: Mechanism 1 (classes, no themechange listener — every panel is rebuilt as fresh HTML on click and static DOM re-resolves via the cascade). Replace the 6 inline literals: faction-bar segments (~L660-666) background:#22c55e/#ef4444/#eab308 → classes bmv1-seg-par / bmv1-seg-pret / bmv1-seg-att; detail-pane stat <strong> colors (~L734-744) → classes bmv1-stat-par / bmv1-stat-pret / bmv1-stat-att (match the file's existing class-prefix style — adjust names to whatever prefix bmv1 already uses, e.g. chip-par family). Add the rules to assets/style.css as an APPEND-ONLY block at the end of the balsojumi/bmv1 section to avoid colliding with the line-level CSS pass: .bmv1-seg-par{background:var(--green)} .bmv1-seg-pret{background:var(--red-bright)} .bmv1-seg-att{background:var(--yellow)} and color: equivalents for the stat classes. IMPORTANT: #ef4444 maps to var(--red-bright), NOT var(--red) — do not merge with #e25b5b. Party/faction colors from DATA.factions[].c stay data-driven (mechanism 3, untouched).

Mechanism 1: drop the '#8b8fa3' fallback at L57 (topicColorFor). Give the chip a CSS default in style.css: .pzv1-row-topic-chip { --topic-color: var(--text-muted); } and have topicColorFor() return '' when the rail button has no data-color, omitting the inline --topic-color in that case so the CSS default wins. Rows re-render on every interaction — no themechange listener. Party colors (c[IDX_PARTY_COLOR], dataset.color) stay brand-driven; flag any AA failures on cream to the palette source in src/render, not here. Note the CSS line-level pass already converts the chip's #8b8fa3 fallbacks at style.css 4442-4449 to var(--text-muted) — keep both changes consistent.

Mechanism 2 (persistent Chart.js bars, created once). Add helper: const cssVar = n => getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue(n).trim(). Wrap both chart constructions in buildTrends(): Chart.defaults.color = cssVar('--text-muted') (replaces '#8b8fa3' L465); Chart.defaults.borderColor = cssVar('--border') (replaces '#2d3148' L466); dataset backgroundColor = cssVar('--blue') (replaces '#3b82f6' L474/498); scales.x.grid.color = cssVar('--border') (L479/503). Store both instances; on document.addEventListener('atmina:themechange', ...) destroy both and call buildTrends() again (charts are tiny — destroy-and-rebuild is the simple correct path; remember Chart.defaults are GLOBAL and must be reassigned before re-creation). Hero carousel script: no change.

Same pattern as index.html.j2 (this surface was missed by the JS audit but flagged by the template audit — persistent Chart.js charts). cssVar helper + factory: Chart.defaults.color = cssVar('--text-muted') (L237), Chart.defaults.borderColor = cssVar('--border') (L238); bar backgroundColor = cssVar('--blue') (L247/264); grid colors = cssVar('--border') (L254/271/301/302); timeline line: borderColor = cssVar('--blue') (L283), area fill = cssVar('--blue') + '1A' (replaces rgba(59,130,246,0.1) L284 — token is 6-digit hex precisely so alpha concat works); second line: cssVar('--green') (L291) + cssVar('--green') + '1A' (L292). Destroy-and-rebuild on 'atmina:themechange'.

JS (mechanism 2): extract chart creation into makeSpark(card) returning the Chart; store instances. DOMAIN_COLORS (L153-154) reads tokens: economy cssVar('--domain-economy'), social cssVar('--domain-social'), prices cssVar('--domain-prices'), state cssVar('--domain-state') — all resolve to 6-digit hex (aliases resolve through getComputedStyle), required for the +'33' gradient alpha concat at L163. Tooltip options: backgroundColor cssVar('--tooltip-bg'), titleColor/bodyColor cssVar('--text'), borderColor cssVar('--tooltip-border') (replaces rgba(22,26,34,0.95)/#e2e4e9/rgba(255,255,255,0.1) L182-184). On 'atmina:themechange': destroy all instances and re-run the factory (canvas createLinearGradient cannot consume CSS vars — gradients must be rebuilt per theme). <style> block tokenization (same commit): L19 hero gradient #fff→var(--stat-hero-grad-start), #a0a5b8→var(--stat-hero-grad-end); drop redundant fallbacks at L14/16/50/82 (var(--accent-highlight, #B71C1C)→var(--accent-highlight)), L29/62 (var(--text, #e2e4e9)→var(--text)), L48 (→var(--domain-color, var(--accent))); L51-54 domain hexes → var(--domain-economy)/var(--domain-social)/var(--domain-prices)/var(--domain-state); L68 .stat-change.positive color #22c55e→var(--green) (its rgba tint bg stays literal); L69 #f87171→var(--red-soft) (rgba(198,40,40,0.15) tint stays literal).

JS (cheapest mechanism-2 case — render() already destroys+recreates): move ALL token reads inside render()/buildAnnotations(), then add document.addEventListener('atmina:themechange', render). Reads: DOMAIN_COLORS (L247-248) → cssVar('--domain-economy'/'--domain-social'/'--domain-prices'/'--domain-state'); CATEGORY_COLORS (L251-252) → crisis '#C62828' stays a literal (theme-neutral), policy cssVar('--yellow'), elections cssVar('--accent'), milestone cssVar('--green'), government cssVar('--orange') — all 6-digit hex for the 'C0'/'40'/'15'/'2E' alpha concats; scatter point borderColor + pointBorderColor '#0d1014' (L281/307) → cssVar('--bg'); tooltip (L316-319) → cssVar('--tooltip-bg'), cssVar('--text') x2, footerColor cssVar('--text-soft'), borderColor cssVar('--tooltip-border'); ticks '#8b8fa3' (L337/342) → cssVar('--text-muted'); grid rgba(255,255,255,0.03) (L338/343) → cssVar('--chart-grid'); axis border rgba(255,255,255,0.05) (L339) → cssVar('--chart-axis'). <style> block: drop var(--text, #e2e4e9) fallbacks (L12/26/33/82/83/108/116/132) and var(--accent, #90A4AE)-style fallbacks (L54/82); L38/39 hero-change → var(--green) / var(--red-soft) (rgba tint bgs stay); L45-48 domain chip TEXT → var(--domain-economy)/var(--domain-social)/var(--orange)/var(--green) (the 0.12-alpha chip bgs stay literal); L56-59 insight border-left → var(--domain-*) equivalents; L85-89 active chip rgba tints stay literal; L90-94 --chip-color: crisis #C62828 stays, policy→var(--yellow), elections→var(--accent), milestone→var(--green), government→var(--orange); L108 rgba(255,255,255,0.04)→var(--overlay-hover).

Owns the WHOLE file (static inline styles + D3) — excluded from template_groups to keep ownership disjoint. STATIC: filter buttons/legend (L32-81): #ef4444→var(--red-bright), #eab308→var(--yellow), #22c55e→var(--green), #8b5cf6→var(--purple); #64748b (shared_topic) stays literal (theme-neutral slate). Legend overlay L59: rgba(15,15,26,0.85)→var(--graph-legend-bg), rgba(255,255,255,0.1)→var(--tooltip-border), rgba(255,255,255,0.6)→var(--graph-label). D3 JS: add cssVar helper; linkColor() (L292-304) reads at call time: spriedze cssVar('--yellow'), uzbrukums cssVar('--red-bright'), support cssVar('--green'), vote agree cssVar('--purple')+'80', vote neutral cssVar('--purple')+'40', vote disagree cssVar('--red-bright')+'4D' (replaces rgba(239,68,68,0.3)), shared_topic rgba(100,116,139,0.35) stays literal, default cssVar('--graph-link-default'). Nodes: fill fallback '#8b8fa3' (L376)→cssVar('--text-muted'); stroke (L377 + deselect resets 436/446/465/474)→cssVar('--graph-node-stroke'); drop-shadow rgba(0,0,0,0.4) (L380)→cssVar('--shadow-overlay'); badge stroke L389 fix stale fallback → var(--bg) (was #0f0f1a); badge count text #fff (L400) stays; labels fill (L411)→cssVar('--graph-label'); hover stroke #fff (L421)→cssVar('--graph-node-stroke-hover'); selected stroke L436/446 fix stale fallback → var(--accent) (was #6366f1). Detail-panel HTML strings: L787/870 #ef4444→var(--red-bright) (use var() directly in the generated inline styles — they live in DOM, cascade applies); L805 rgba(255,255,255,0.02)→var(--surface-lift) (note: its var(--border-soft) reference starts WORKING once the token exists — eyeball it); L869/932/934 #8b5cf6→var(--purple); L896 #64748b stays; L932/963 ternaries → var(--purple)/var(--red-bright)/var(--yellow)/var(--green). THEMECHANGE: on 'atmina:themechange' re-apply the attr() styling functions to existing link/node/label selections (or re-run the style-application pass); detail panels rebuild on click anyway. partyColors map (L239-248) is BRAND data — leave; centralizing it server-side is a backlog item.

No change (mechanism 3, confirmed by JS audit): sgv1 renders only data-driven brand colors and rebuilds per keystroke; x-v1/pnv1/spriedzes scripts toggle display/classes only. Listed so nothing looks forgotten.

tokenPlan.switch_spec:
PLACEMENT + CHROME CONSTRAINTS (load-bearing): the toggle lives INSIDE the existing
in templates/base.html.j2, as a direct child after .nav-links and before #nav-burger (right edge). Its JS goes INSIDE the existing single chrome — wrapped in try/catch for privacy modes; sets data-theme ONLY when saved==='light' (dark stays the attribute-less default). Curated pages have FROZEN heads and never receive this script — so ALSO repeat the same one-liner (idempotent) at the TOP of the chrome
open_questions: Curated frozen pages (curated/atmina/finanses.html + statistika/ overlay): content keeps ~110 dark-tuned inline styles and a frozen <head> (stale ?v= cache-bust, no FOUC bootstrap). Options: (a) accept late-applied half-dark light mode there short-term, (b) force data-theme dark on curated pages, or (c) re-freeze the snapshots once after the theme lands (recommended). Which?

Legacy blue family: the rgba(59,130,246,*) tints in style.css (multi-select selection, table-scroll glow, matrix row/toggle tints, badge-blue) stay literal blue in both themes per the audit's same_in_light marks, and --blue stays a real blue (#3b82f6 / #1d4ed8) rather than remapping to the navy accent. Confirm blue remains a distinct 'info' hue, or schedule a follow-up remap to the accent family.
--purple folds #a855f7 (lēmumprojekts pill, style.css 5022) into #8b5cf6 — a small visible DARK-mode shift on one pill type. Approve, or add a second purple token to keep dark pixel-identical?

Defining --border-soft activates two previously-broken var(--border-soft) references (style.css:514 .prv2-context border-left, currently falling back to currentColor; saites detail-panel konteksta piezīme, currently colorless). Both will change appearance in DARK mode too — eyeball both and approve the new look.

Image/logo audit: the palette auditor read docs/featured-images as cream engravings (so --media-backdrop light = #efe8d6 cream letterbox), but the CSS auditor claimed they are dark-generated. Verify actual featured/hero images and the nav/footer logo SVG on the cream background before release — the logo may need a light-mode variant.

Warm shadows: the palette mandates umber shadows (rgba(63,54,38,*)) in light mode, so the plan tokenizes the rgba(0,0,0,*) shadow literals even though the CSS audit marked them same_in_light. Dark values are preserved byte-exact. Confirm the warm-shadow direction (the alternative — leaving shadows black — needs no tokens).

color-mix() is used for all --bg/--surface2/--border/--accent/--accent-brand/--accent-highlight alpha scrims (Baseline-supported since 2023). On older browsers an unsupported color-mix makes the declaration invalid — the sticky nav would lose its background. If legacy support matters, instruct the CSS pass to double-declare the two critical surfaces (nav L86, mobile nav L5356): literal first, color-mix second.
Weekly-brief paper block: #333 blockquote text (style.css 5181) is near-unreadable on the CURRENT dark theme (pre-existing, fine on paper). Fix now as a drive-by or leave for BACKLOG?

Party/topic brand palettes (sg-index.json, saites partyColors, party_color columns) stay theme-neutral by design; the planned Playwright AA scan may flag some hexes on cream — failures should be fixed at the palette source in src/render (and the saites JS map centralized server-side). Pre-approve that follow-up scope?

Cache-bust gap flagged by the risks audit: _resolve_assets_version only hashes style.css/pzv1.js/sgv1.js mtimes, but bmv1.js (which this work edits) ships under ?v= too — a follow-up bmv1-only fix would serve stale JS. Add bmv1.js/pnv1.js/x-v1.js to the versioned list as a separate intentional commit during this work?

The prefers-reduced-motion blocks at style.css L689-695 and L5701-5704 contain color overrides not enumerated by the audit (the L1874-1879 gold one is theme-neutral). Implementation must eyeball both blocks in both themes — flagging here so it is not lost.

tokens.name: --bg
tokens.note: Featured-image cream paper, lightened one step from the #f4efe4 anchor for UI comfort (large fills read warmer than print). Still unmistakably the brand paper.

tokens.name: --surface
tokens.note: Exact brand paper from .weekly-stat-card (style.css ~5173). Cards sit as 'aged sheets' slightly deeper than the page — light mode inverts dark mode's lighter-is-elevated; depth comes from tone + shadow.

tokens.name: --surface2
tokens.note: Deeper aged-paper for nested chrome (tabs, code, table headers). Text 13.15:1, muted 5.52:1 on it — both AA.

tokens.name: --text
tokens.note: Warm ink near-black (not pure #000) matching the engraving line work in the featured images. 15.46:1 on bg.

tokens.name: --text-muted
tokens.note: Warm sepia-gray ink. 6.49:1 on bg and 5.52:1 even on the darkest surface2 — AA everywhere it can land.

tokens.name: --accent
tokens.note: Link/interactive color: a mid ink-navy derived from brand #1f2d4d, lightened just enough to be visually distinct from body text while keeping 8.93:1. a:hover already swaps to --accent-highlight (#B71C1C), which also passes.

tokens.name: --accent-brand
tokens.note: The weekly-brief ink navy becomes the brand chrome color in light mode (border-left bars, badges, .fact-card accents). 12.29:1 on bg; as a fill it carries #f4efe4 text exactly like .weekly-badge does today.

tokens.name: --accent-highlight
tokens.note: Brand red kept verbatim per constraint. 5.93:1 on bg, 5.73:1 on surface — AA for body text in both placements.

tokens.name: --green
tokens.note: Recognizably green (not forest-black). 4.71:1 on surface, 4.87:1 on bg — AA.

tokens.name: --yellow
tokens.note: Golden mustard — the darkest hue family to make AA while still reading yellow. 4.65:1 on surface. If a future use needs more headroom, #806000 gives 5.10:1 at slight vividness cost.

tokens.name: --red
tokens.note: Status red, warmer/brighter than brand crimson #B71C1C so the two stay distinguishable side by side. 5.73:1 on surface.

tokens.name: --orange
tokens.note: Burnt orange, clearly distinct from --red and --yellow. 4.85:1 on surface, 5.01:1 on bg.

tokens.name: --border
tokens.note: Decorative hairline in paper-edge tone (the deckled border in thread-2026-05-29-2.png). Intentionally low-contrast; never the sole affordance.

tokens.name: --border-strong
tokens.note: NEW token for interactive component outlines (inputs, focused chips, toggles) where WCAG 1.4.11 wants 3:1 — 3.26:1 on bg. Dark value is a lifted step of #2d3148.

tokens.name: --nav-backdrop
tokens.note: Mirrors the existing dark nav (style.css:86, rgba(13,16,20,0.92) + blur(12px)); same alpha and blur, cream base.

tokens.name: --shadow-color
tokens.note: Warm umber ink shadow instead of neutral black — paper casts warm shadows. Substitute into existing offsets, e.g. 0 12px 32px var(--shadow-color).

tokens.name: --surface-hover
tokens.note: NEW hover tint between surface and surface2. Subtle by design; pair with border darkening to #c2b692 on hover so the state change is not color-only.

tokens.name: --accent-brand-hover
tokens.note: Navy deepens on hover in light mode (lightens in dark). 14.43:1 on bg.

tokens.name: --accent-highlight-hover
tokens.note: Red deepens on hover/active in light mode. 8.13:1 on bg.

tokens.name: --switch-plate
tokens.note: Toggle wall-plate: one step deeper than bg so it reads as a physical object resting on the paper, like the table in dailies/2026-05-18.png.

tokens.name: --switch-outline
tokens.note: The 2px editorial ink stroke around plate, lever and screws — black ink line on paper in light mode, pale paper line on dark (inverted engraving).

tokens.name: --switch-lever
tokens.note: Lever body: solid ink black on cream plate (14.03:1) in light; cream paper lever on dark plate (~14.7:1) in dark. Reads like the black chairs/figures in the featured images.

tokens.name: --switch-lever-detail
tokens.note: Lever slot/highlight line — the opposite ink, giving the engraved bevel effect.

tokens.name: --switch-screw
tokens.note: Screw-head fill; stroke + 45-degree slot line use --switch-outline. Two screws, slots at differing angles, for the hand-drawn editorial feel.

paletteAudit.rationale: Sources read: featured images docs/featured-images/dailies/2026-05-18.png (cream paper field, solid ink-black serif headline, black-filled furniture with fine engraved line work) and thread-2026-05-30-1.png / thread-2026-05-29-2.png (sepia engravings with deckled paper borders); the existing in-site paper palette at assets/style.css:5160-5182 (.weekly-badge / .weekly-stat-card: #f4efe4 paper + #1f2d4d ink navy); and the dark :root at assets/style.css:29-45. Anchoring: --bg is #f7f3e8, one comfort step lighter than the #f4efe4 anchor, and the exact brand paper #f4efe4 becomes --surface, so every card literally IS the weekly-stat-card paper. Light mode inverts dark mode's elevation direction (surfaces get deeper, not lighter), which matches layered-paper physically; --shadow-color is warm umber rather than neutral black for the same reason. Text is warm ink #1f1b14 (not #000) to match the engraving ink. Both mandated brand accents are kept: #1f2d4d takes over --accent-brand (replacing slate #37474F, which has no meaning on cream) and #B71C1C stays as --accent-highlight, passing AA on both bg and surface; --accent (links) is #2c4270, a navy light enough to be visibly a link next to body text yet 8.9:1. Status colors are darkened to the most saturated value in each hue family that still clears 4.5:1 on --surface (the worst-case lighter placement of the two main backdrops; all also pass on --bg): green #1a7a3a, mustard-gold #8a6500, warm red #b42318 (kept hue-distinct from brand crimson #B71C1C), burnt orange #b8400c. All ratios computed with the WCAG relative-luminance formula via Python (no files written; transcript shows the runs). The one intentional non-pass is the decorative hairline --border (paper-edge tone, exempt under WCAG 1.4.11 as it is never the sole indicator); the new --border-strong #948659 (3.26:1) covers interactive outlines. The toggle switch is drawn in the images' ink-outline style: in light mode a cream plate (#efe8d6) one step deeper than bg with a 2px black ink stroke, solid ink-black lever with a cream slot highlight, and ink-outlined screws with angled slots; in dark mode the engraving inverts - near-bg plate #1b202b, pale-paper stroke #e8e2d2, cream #f4efe4 lever. Implementation note for the integrator: html[data-theme="light"] only needs to override the color tokens; --radius/--transition are theme-independent, and hardcoded dark rgba(13,16,20,...) overlays (style.css:2995,3043,3093,5356) plus rgba(0,0,0,...) shadows should be migrated to --nav-backdrop / --shadow-color to theme correctly.

test_dependencies.what: SHA-256 byte-baselines for ~16 page families (pretrunas, personas/politiki, partijas, pozicijas/zinas/statistika, balsojumi/likumprojekti/likumi, x, spriedzes/saites, analizes/sintezes, blog, index.html). Session fixture full-renders the committed fixture DB under freezegun (FREEZE_INSTANT 2026-06-01) with ATMINA_ASSETS_VERSION pinned to 'test' and compares against tests/fixtures/render_baseline_*.json.
test_dependencies.risk: CERTAIN breakage: any byte change to base.html.j2 (nav toggle, data-theme attr on <html>, head script) or to any of the 14 templates flips EVERY page hash -> all baseline tests fail. Expected workflow, not a bug: `REGEN=1 pytest tests/test_render_chars.py`, re-run without REGEN, commit the regenerated fixture JSONs alongside the template change. Note the inverse: a style.css-ONLY change does NOT drift baselines (assets_version is env-pinned and CSS bytes are not hashed), so CSS and template edits can be verified separately.

test_dependencies.what: test_copy_curated_resyncs_chrome_from_base asserts the chrome injected into curated pages contains exact markers: 'id="nav-burger"', 'class="nav-more"', 'href="temas.html"', 'href="mediji.html"', 'href="finanses.html" class="active"', 'class="footer-brand"', '>Par mums<', about.html/kontakti.html links, '../'-prefixed variants for statistika/ depth, and that page content + trailing <script>PAGE-SCRIPT</script> survive. Other tests lock _heavy_fetch_plan and image-copy suffix rules (theme-irrelevant).
test_dependencies.risk: HIGH if nav/footer structure changes: renaming class="nav"/"footer", removing the burger or 'Vairak' disclosure, or dropping the asserted links fails the test. Worse, src/render/_orchestrator.py _CHROME_SPECS regex requires `<nav class="nav">...</nav>` followed only by whitespace then ONE `<script>...</script>` — inserting markup between </nav> and that script, or adding a second adjacent <script>, either raises RuntimeError at build time (_base_chrome_blocks) or silently leaves the new script out of curated pages. ADDING a theme-toggle button inside the existing <nav> and its JS inside the existing chrome <script> keeps both test and build green.

test_dependencies.what: Builds its OWN Jinja Environment mirroring the orchestrator's filters/globals (lv_date, safe_json, safe_url, autolink_bills, image_variant, lv_plural, _party_short_name, bill_slugs, assets_version='test'), renders temas pages (which extend base.html.j2) and asserts file existence + content substrings.
test_dependencies.risk: MEDIUM: if base.html.j2 starts using a NEW custom Jinja filter, this hand-built env raises TemplateAssertionError (unknown filter). A new GLOBAL (e.g. a theme flag) silently renders empty (default Undefined) — no failure, but possibly wrong output. Keep new chrome logic limited to existing context vars (assets_prefix, active_page, assets_version) or update this env in the same commit.

test_dependencies.what: Same pattern as test_topics.py: private Jinja env with assets_version='test' (line 117), renders politician profile pages through base.html.j2, asserts Latvian content labels ('Pieminēts medijos' etc.), not chrome.
test_dependencies.risk: MEDIUM, same filter-registration coupling as test_topics.py; content assertions themselves are unaffected by nav/CSS changes.

test_dependencies.what: test_likumi_index_html_generated_by_full_pipeline runs a FULL generate_public_site() against the LIVE data/atmina.db, writing into the real ./output/ tree, then asserts content substrings in likumi.html and balsojumi.html ('Pamatlikumi', specific hrefs).
test_dependencies.risk: LOW for nav-only changes (asserts page content, not chrome), but it is the test that will fail LOUDLY on any Jinja syntax error in base.html.j2 or the 14 templates, and it makes every pytest run mutate the live output/ directory — don't be surprised when output/atmina/ changes mid-development.

test_dependencies.what: Renders bill detail pages and asserts Latvian empty-state strings ('Iesniedzējs nav reģistrēts', 'Stadiju nav reģistrētas') in the output HTML.
test_dependencies.risk: LOW: content-substring assertions; only breaks on template syntax errors or if those strings/templates are restructured.

test_dependencies.what: test_heavy_fetch_plan / test_narrow_render_skips_unneeded_heavy_fetches lock the --only domain dependency map; _copy_brief_images/_copy_images/_copy_curated tests lock asset-copy semantics.
test_dependencies.risk: NONE from theming unless you add a new render domain or new deployable asset suffix; listed for completeness because they import src.render._orchestrator whose module-level _CHROME_SPECS regexes execute at import.

test_dependencies.what: test_matcher.py, test_briefs.py, test_analyze.py, test_dashboard_*.py, test_graphics_*.py, test_quality.py, test_lv_style.py etc. matched the grep only because 'nav' is the Latvian word for 'is not' in docstrings/strings, or 'footer' refers to daily-brief footer STATS dicts (test_generate.py, test_blog_footer_batch.py), not page chrome.
test_dependencies.risk: NONE — no dependency on site chrome, style.css, or assets_version.

render_pipeline_notes: check.sh (~/atmina/scripts/check.sh) runs exactly three steps with the resolved .venv python (walks up parent dirs for worktrees): (1) `ruff check src scripts tests`, (2) `pytest tests -q`, (3) a generate_public_site smoke defaulting to `only={dashboard,blog}` (override via CHECK_RENDER_ONLY env, 'all' for full) that writes into the REAL output/ dir. set -e — first failure aborts.

Propagation of a base.html.j2 nav change + new CSS is complete and automatic per full build: (a) src/render/_orchestrator.py lines 316-325 copies every file in assets/ (style.css, bmv1.js, pzv1.js, sgv1.js, pnv1.js, x-v1.js...) into output/atmina/assets/ via shutil.copy2 on EVERY build; (b) all template-driven pages re-render from base.html.j2 with env.globals['assets_version'] = _resolve_assets_version(); (c) curated frozen pages (curated/atmina/finanses.html + statistika/*) get their <nav>+following chrome <script> and <footer> re-rendered from base.html.j2 at copy time via _copy_curated -> _sync_curated_chrome -> _base_chrome_blocks/_rendered_chrome — so the new nav (incl. a theme toggle placed inside <nav> or inside the chrome script) reaches curated pages too.

Chrome caching: _base_chrome_blocks is @lru_cache(maxsize=1) and _rendered_chrome @lru_cache(maxsize=16) — PROCESS-scoped only. CLI renders are fresh processes and the ops dashboard does not render in-process (it only shells out to scripts/deploy.sh), so no stale chrome persists across builds. The only hazard is a long-lived Python session (REPL/pytest) rendering before and after a base.html.j2 edit — it will inject the OLD cached chrome into curated pages.

CRITICAL constraint on the curated chrome fragment: _rendered_chrome renders it through a BARE Environment(autoescape=True) with ONLY assets_prefix and active_page in context (_orchestrator.py lines 158, 184-189). If the theme toggle markup inside <nav> references {{ assets_version }} or any other global, it renders as EMPTY STRING on curated pages (silent divergence); if it uses a custom filter (lv_date etc.), the build raises TemplateAssertionError. Keep the nav fragment self-contained.

Curated-page <head> is FROZEN and NOT resynced: curated/atmina/finanses.html line 21 hard-codes `assets/style.css?v=1776544394`. After the theme ships, curated pages serve the NEW style.css bytes (file is overwritten in assets/) but under the OLD ?v= query — returning visitors with a cached copy get STALE CSS on finanses/statistika pages. Likewise, any <head>-level theme bootstrap script (the localStorage data-theme FOUC guard) will NEVER reach curated pages — they would stay default-dark / never honor the user's light choice. Mitigation: either put the data-theme bootstrap inside the chrome <script> block that _sync_curated_chrome already injects, or extend _CHROME_SPECS with a head fragment, or re-freeze the curated snapshots once after the theme lands.

Cache-bust gap: _resolve_assets_version (src/render/_common.py:1048-1072) takes max(mtime) over ONLY style.css, pzv1.js, sgv1.js. bmv1.js, pnv1.js and x-v1.js are loaded with ?v={{ assets_version }} (balsojumi.html.j2:251, personas.html.j2:186, x.html.j2:161) but their mtimes do NOT bump the version. This theme change touches style.css simultaneously so the initial deploy busts everything, but any FOLLOW-UP bmv1.js-only fix (including via scripts/render_balsojumi_only.py, which copies bmv1.js+style.css but reuses _resolve_assets_version) ships under an unchanged ?v= — returning visitors run stale JS. Flagging per operating principle 1: consider adding bmv1.js/pnv1.js/x-v1.js to the versioned list as part of this work (separate, intentional commit).

Canonical full-site regeneration (wiki/operations/commands.md): `.venv/Scripts/python.exe -c "from src.render import generate_public_site; generate_public_site()"` or equivalently `.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m src.render` with no --only (~3 min; `src.generate` is a legacy re-export shim). Narrow renders: `python -m src.render --only=DOMAIN1,DOMAIN2` (~30s-2min). Because a base.html.j2 chrome change touches EVERY page, this release requires the FULL render before deploy — a narrow --only build would leave un-listed domains serving old chrome with new CSS (consistent with the standing feedback_render_narrow_scope rule: full path for release/baseline). Deploy via `bash scripts/deploy.sh` (--dry-run to preview; --no-delete is the standing default).

PNG-render isolation CONFIRMED: templates/og-card.html.j2 carries its own inline <style> block + a Google-fonts <link> only; templates/social/quote_card.html.j2 likewise has only an inline <style> (line 5). Neither links assets/style.css. src/social_agent/visuals.py references style.css ONLY in a comment (line 7: brand tokens 'must match' — hard-coded duplicates, not parsed). Both card templates are standalone HTML documents with their own <html> element, so an html[data-theme="light"] selector in style.css cannot leak into PNG renders. Caveat: their hard-coded dark brand tokens will visually DIVERGE from a light site — acceptable (social cards staying dark is a brand choice), but note scripts/render_intro_thread_v2.py also mirrors the palette in comments.

No Python parses or string-matches style.css CONTENT anywhere in the repo (verified repo-wide *.py grep): the only touchpoints are an mtime stat in _resolve_assets_version, file-copy-by-name in _orchestrator.py and scripts/render_balsojumi_only.py, and comments (visuals.py, render_intro_thread_v2.py, test_render_chars.py docstring). CSS can be rewritten freely without breaking any Python reader.
other_risks: Inline <style> blocks exist in 8 templates (blog-post.html.j2, politician.html.j2, kontakti.html.j2, statistika-detail.html.j2, about.html.j2, statistika.html.j2 + the two intentionally-standalone card templates). Any hard-coded dark hex colors there bypass :root CSS variables and will NOT respond to html[data-theme="light"] — audit each during implementation and migrate page-specific colors to var(--*) tokens, or those pages will render half-dark in light mode.

JS-generated inline colors: assets/bmv1.js emits hard-coded `background:#22c55e/#ef4444/#eab308` and `color:#...` inline styles (lines 660-742, vote bars/stats) and assets/pzv1.js has a fallback '#8b8fa3' dot color (line 57) plus data-color values injected server-side from templates. Inline styles defeat CSS-variable theming — verify legibility on a paper background or switch to classes/CSS vars; neither file currently reads data-theme or getComputedStyle, so no JS will adapt automatically.

curated/atmina/finanses.html contains ~110 inline style= attributes with dark-tuned colors in its FROZEN content (only nav/footer are resynced). Under the light theme these pages keep dark-styled content blocks. Decide explicitly: accept curated pages as dark-only (force data-theme=dark on them), or re-freeze the snapshots with theme-aware markup.

statistika pages have TWO producers — generate_statistika (renders from data/csp.db via statistika*.html.j2 templates) AND the curated/atmina/statistika/ overlay copied by _copy_curated. Ordering between them inside a given build/run was not fully traced in this audit; when testing the theme on /statistika*, verify which producer's output actually wins in output/atmina/ before concluding theming works there.

Workflow sequencing for the change itself: land template edits -> run `REGEN=1 pytest tests/test_render_chars.py` -> plain pytest -> bash scripts/check.sh -> FULL render -> deploy. Committing template changes without the regenerated render_baseline_*.json fixtures leaves CI/check.sh red for the next session.
The theme toggle introduces new user-facing Latvian UI strings (aria-labels, 'Gaišais/Tumšais režīms' etc.) — the CLAUDE.md LV grammar + stylistics gate applies to them before commit/publish.

test_render_chars.py REGEN note: the blog/likumprojekti page COUNTS in baselines are dynamic against the committed fixture DB — REGEN only after the template work is final, and do not REGEN while the working tree also has unrelated drift (the current git status shows many untracked files but no tracked-file modifications, so baselines should regenerate cleanly).

surfaces.mechanism: innerHTML string templates. Vote cells, badges, chips use CSS classes (vote-par, badge-green, faction-chip, chip-par...) — already cascade-themed. Hardcoded hex appears only in inline style attributes inside the detail pane: faction-bar segments (lines ~660-666: background:#22c55e / #ef4444 / #eab308) and politician stat <strong> colors (lines ~734-744: color:#22c55e / #ef4444 / #eab308). Party/faction colors come from JSON (f.c) and are set as --filter-color, .filter-dot background, --faction-color (lines ~183-188, 487).
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (1): replace the 6 inline literals with classes — e.g. .faction-bar-seg.seg-par/.seg-pret/.seg-att and .detail-stat strong.stat-par/.stat-pret/.stat-att — styled in style.css via var(--green)/var(--red)/var(--yellow) (note #ef4444 != the existing --red #e25b5b; pick one token). No themechange listener needed: every panel is rebuilt as fresh HTML on click, and the detail pane survives a toggle only as static DOM whose classes re-resolve via the cascade. Party colors (f.c): leave as-is, brand colors (mechanism 3).

surfaces.mechanism: DOM building per row. Three inline-style assignments: (a) topicColorFor() fallback literal "#8b8fa3" (line 57) fed into style="--topic-color:..." on the topic chip; (b) row hover row.style.borderLeftColor = c[IDX_PARTY_COLOR] (lines 215-216) — party brand; (c) party short label style="color:${partyColor}" (line 230) — party brand; (d) rail rows b.style.borderLeftColor = b.dataset.color (lines 369/388/453) — topic/party colors from server-rendered data-color attrs.
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (1) for the only literal: drop the "#8b8fa3" JS fallback — give .pzv1-row-topic-chip a CSS default `--topic-color: var(--text-muted);` in style.css and have topicColorFor() return empty string when the rail button has no data-color (omit the inline --topic-color in that case). Rows re-render on every interaction, so no listener needed. Party and topic palette colors: leave (mechanism 3), but flag for the Playwright contrast scan — some party hexes may fail AA on cream paper; if so, fix at the palette source (src/render), not in this file.

surfaces.mechanism: Typeahead dropdown rows built as HTML strings; only color usage is inline style="--pc: <partyColor>" on avatars (lines 128-129) and style="background: <topic/party color>" on .sgv1-dot (lines 134, 140, 147). All values come from data/sg-index.json (P_COLOR, T_COLOR, G_COLOR).
colors: row[P_COLOR], row[T_COLOR], row[G_COLOR] — data-driven party/topic brand colors only; zero literals
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (3): leave as-is. All chrome (drop background, kicker, hover) lives in style.css under .sgv1-* and themes via the cascade. Dropdown is rebuilt on every keystroke, so even brand-dot rendering self-heals after a toggle.

surfaces.mechanism: Filter logic only — el.style.display toggles and class add/remove (active, selected, open, has-selection). No color assignment of any kind.
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (3): no change. All visual states are classes themed in style.css.

surfaces.mechanism: Filter/sort logic only — display toggles, is-active class toggles, chip text. No color assignment.

Inline <script>: one persistent Chart.js line sparkline per card, created once on DOMContentLoaded, never recreated. Colors in: DOMAIN_COLORS map (dataset borderColor + pointBackgroundColor), ctx.createLinearGradient canvas gradient (color+'33' → 'transparent') as dataset backgroundColor, and options.plugins.tooltip {backgroundColor 'rgba(22,26,34,0.95)', titleColor/bodyColor '#e2e4e9', borderColor 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)'}. Scales are display:false (no grid/tick colors). The template <style> block also carries literals (h1 gradient #fff→#a0a5b8, .stat-change #22c55e/#f87171 + rgba bgs, var() fallbacks #B71C1C/#e2e4e9, domain hexes).
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (2). Add cssVar(n){return getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue(n).trim()}; build tooltip colors from tokens (bg: --surface-ish overlay token, title/body: --text, border: --border). Keep DOMAIN_COLORS as 6-digit-hex tokens (--domain-economy etc., required 6-digit because of the +'33' alpha concat) so the light block can darken them per spec. Extract chart creation into makeSpark(card) returning the Chart; store instances; on document 'atmina:themechange' loop instances: re-read tokens, rebuild the canvas gradient (createLinearGradient cannot consume CSS vars), reassign dataset.borderColor/backgroundColor/pointBackgroundColor + tooltip options, chart.update('none'). Simpler equivalent: destroy all and re-run the factory. Separately tokenize the <style>-block literals during the CSS pass.

surfaces.mechanism: Inline <script>: one Chart.js line chart + annotation plugin. render() destroys-and-recreates chartInstance but runs only once at load; category toggles/legend hover mutate options.plugins.annotation.annotations then chart.update('none'). Colors in: DOMAIN_COLORS + CATEGORY_COLORS maps (annotation line borderColor with hex-alpha suffixes 'C0'/'15'/'40', annotation point backgroundColor, dataset borderColor/pointBackgroundColor), canvas gradient color+'2E', pointBorderColor and annotation point borderColor '#0d1014' (= dark --bg), tooltip {rgba(13,16,20,0.96), #e2e4e9 ×2, footerColor #c9cdd9, border rgba(255,255,255,0.1)}, scales x/y ticks color '#8b8fa3' (= dark --text-muted), grid 'rgba(255,255,255,0.03)', x border 'rgba(255,255,255,0.05)'. Template <style> block also has many literals (chips, legend hover rgba(255,255,255,0.04), var() fallbacks).
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (2), cheapest possible because render() already destroys+recreates: move all token reads inside render()/buildAnnotations() (pointBorderColor and annotation point border → var(--bg); ticks → --text-muted; grid/axis → a new --chart-grid token since white-alpha inverts badly on paper; tooltip → --text/--border + surface token), then add document.addEventListener('atmina:themechange', render). buildAnnotations() runs inside render so annotations re-theme for free. Keep DOMAIN_COLORS/CATEGORY_COLORS as 6-digit hex tokens (alpha concat 'C0'/'40'/'15'/'2E' requires it) with darkened light-theme values per spec.

surfaces.mechanism: Two inline <script> blocks (tab switching + topic filter) assign only style.display — zero colors; no Chart.js. The color surface here is template-side: the Saites mini-graph inline SVG uses presentation attributes — circle stroke="#fff" on center + neighbor nodes (lines 599, 604), fill from party colors with Jinja fallback '#8b8fa3' (line 591); text fills already use var(--text-muted). Many prv2/badge colors come via classes + style="--pc/background: {{ c.party_color }}" (brand).
surfaces.plan: JS: no change needed (mechanism 3). SVG: mechanism (1) — SVG presentation attributes lose to CSS, so add class="saites-node" to both <circle> elements, drop stroke="#fff", and style .mini-saites-graph .saites-node { stroke: var(--bg); } in style.css (stroke's job is to separate the node from the page background, so --bg is the correct token). Optionally swap the '#8b8fa3' Jinja fallback to a class-based default the same way; party fills stay brand (mechanism 3).

surfaces.mechanism: Inline <script> (only when trends_data): two persistent Chart.js horizontal bar charts (topicsChart, politiciansChart), created once on DOMContentLoaded, never recreated. Colors: global Chart.defaults.color='#8b8fa3' and Chart.defaults.borderColor='#2d3148' (drive tick + default borders), dataset backgroundColor '#3b82f6' (×2), options.scales.x.grid.color '#2d3148'. Second inline script (hero carousel) is class-toggling only — no colors. sgv1.js is included here but audited separately.
colors: #3b82f6 (bar fill accent blue — not in the :root palette)
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (2). Wrap chart construction in buildTrends(): read cssVar('--text-muted') / cssVar('--border') into Chart.defaults and scales.x.grid.color, and a token for the bar fill (suggest new --chart-bar; #3b82f6 reads fine on paper too, so mechanism-3 leave-as-is is defensible — but the grid/ticks MUST re-theme). Keep the two instances; on document 'atmina:themechange' either mutate defaults + per-chart options/dataset then chart.update('none'), or destroy both and call buildTrends() again (simplest, charts are tiny). Hero carousel script: no change.

surfaces.mechanism: Inline <script>: multi-select filter logic — style.display toggles and selected/open/has-selection class toggles only. Badge colors (badge-red/yellow/green) and var(--text-muted) inline styles are emitted by Jinja in markup, themed via CSS classes/tokens.
surfaces.plan: Mechanism (3): no JS change. Covered entirely by the style.css token pass.

jsAudit.notes: Scope verified against the approved spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-13-light-theme-design.md (which already mandates the getComputedStyle + 'atmina:themechange' pattern for bmv1.js/pzv1.js/Chart.js; the event does not exist anywhere in assets/templates yet — only the ops dashboard at src/dashboard has its own separate toggle). Existing :root tokens to anchor against (assets/style.css:29-44): --bg #0d1014, --surface #161a22, --surface2, --text #e2e4e9, --text-muted #8b8fa3, --accent #90A4AE, --accent-brand #37474F, --accent-highlight #B71C1C, --green #22c55e, --yellow #eab308, --red #e25b5b, --orange #f97316, --border #2d3148. Note #ef4444/#f87171 (used in bmv1.js and statistika templates) do NOT match --red #e25b5b — the token pass should unify or add a second red token. Chart.js specifics: (a) statistika.html.j2 sparklines and index.html.j2 trend bars are PERSISTENT (created once, never recreated) → need either in-place option mutation + chart.update() or a destroy-and-rebuild factory on themechange; (b) statistika-detail.html.j2 already has a destroy-and-recreate render() — one addEventListener('atmina:themechange', render) plus moving token reads inside render() is the entire fix; (c) canvas gradients (createLinearGradient in both statistika templates) cannot consume CSS vars and must be rebuilt in JS per theme; (d) hex-alpha concatenation (color+'33'/'2E'/'C0'/'40'/'15') requires any new chart tokens to hold 6-digit hex values; (e) index.html.j2 mutates GLOBAL Chart.defaults — re-theming must reassign those before per-chart updates. Color-holding Chart.js option paths: data.datasets[N].{borderColor,backgroundColor,pointBackgroundColor,pointBorderColor}; options.plugins.tooltip.{backgroundColor,titleColor,bodyColor,footerColor,borderColor}; options.plugins.annotation.annotations[k].{borderColor,backgroundColor} (detail page); options.scales.{x,y}.{ticks.color,grid.color,border.color}; Chart.defaults.{color,borderColor}. Out of JS scope but found while auditing and needed for the CSS pass: the <style> blocks in statistika.html.j2 / statistika-detail.html.j2 carry ~30 literals (white-alpha grids, rgba chip backgrounds, h1 #fff→#a0a5b8 text gradient, var() fallbacks) — white-alpha overlays invert badly on cream paper and need dedicated tokens (--chart-grid, --overlay-hover), not just value swaps. Party/topic/faction brand colors flowing from JSON (bmv1 f.c, pzv1 IDX_PARTY_COLOR/data-color, sgv1 P/T/G_COLOR, sg-index.json, politician/index party_color) are deliberately left theme-neutral per mechanism (3); the spec's Playwright WCAG scan should confirm they pass AA on #f4efe4, with any failures fixed at the palette source in src/render, not per-surface.
